Incoming Test Engineer Intern · Analog Devices Inc. · Summer 2026

Building hardware
from the ground up.

FPGA RTL in Verilog, bare-metal firmware in C, and multi-layer PCB layouts in Altium Designer. 3rd-year Electrical & Computer Engineering at UofT · 2× Dean's Honour List.

View Projects GitHub LinkedIn Contact
ABOUT

I'm an Electrical & Computer Engineering student at the University of Toronto, on the 2× Dean's Honour List, with a focus on the hardware side of computing — RTL design, embedded firmware, and PCB layout.

I'm drawn to the intersection of hardware and software: writing firmware that runs bare-metal on a microcontroller, routing the PCB that carries it, or designing the Verilog that describes the logic underneath it all.

This summer I'm joining Analog Devices Inc. as a Test Engineer Intern — working with analog and mixed-signal ICs on the Quality and Optimization Excellence team.

Outside coursework: electronics team at UofT Formula SAE, and a Minor in Artificial Intelligence.

PROJECTS

Custom Embedded Controller — ECE295

Full embedded system PCB designed from schematic to Gerbers in Altium Designer. Integrates a microcontroller with I²C, UART, and JTAG peripherals across a dual-layer board.

Altium DesignerPCB LayoutI²C
GitHub

Wayout — FPGA First-Person Maze Game

Real-time pseudo-3D maze game on DE1-SoC, built in Verilog with a full VGA graphics pipeline and FSM-driven game logic — delivered over a 3-week sprint.

VerilogDE1-SoCModelSim
GitHub

5-Stage CPU Pipeline Simulator

Cycle-accurate software model of a 5-stage RISC pipeline with RAW hazard detection, stall injection, and per-cycle performance analytics.

PythonNumPy / PandasComputer Architecture
GitHub

EXPERIENCE
Analog Devices Inc.
Summer 2026
Internship
Incoming
Test Engineer Intern — Quality & Optimization Excellence
  • Joining ADI's Quality and Optimization Excellence team to work on test engineering for analog and mixed-signal ICs — one of the world's leading semiconductor companies for precision measurement and signal processing.
UofT Formula SAE Racing
Sept. 2025 – Present
Toronto, ON
Electronics Team Member — PCB Design
  • Designed a PCB-based LED driver circuit in Altium Designer — full end-to-end flow: schematic capture, PCB layout, and design verification.
  • Performed electrical design calculations for current limiting and component derating to ensure reliable operation within device specifications.
  • Built custom schematic symbols and PCB footprints; integrated vendor STEP models to validate mechanical fit and board-level assembly.
  • Produced manufacturing-ready deliverables: Gerber files, pick-and-place outputs, and structured BOM for fabrication and assembly review.
Provectus Networks
Nov. 2022 – Mar. 2023
Vancouver, BC
IT Engineering Intern
  • Upgraded 20+ client workstations by replacing DDR3 RAM and hardware components, reducing boot and load times by up to 35%.
  • Diagnosed and resolved hardware failures, driver conflicts, and OS errors across diverse hardware setups.
CONTACT

Open to PEY co-op and internship roles in embedded systems, firmware, FPGA/RTL design, IC test, and hardware verification — 2026–2027.
